receipt.narrative:
RE : Bread XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  I received a denial for unclear reasons on numerous occasions. 
It appears that it was due to a purported inability in verifying identity. 
The adverse action letter vaguely states, " Unable to verify identity '', however, the lender does not specify any details or allow a way to reconsider the decision. 
The XXXX just directed me to wait 30 days and then reapply. 
I did so multiple times with the exact same outcome. 
In fact, I even submitted a notarized statement of identity - all to no avail! 
Federal law requires that consumers have a right to fair and transparent lending practices. 
I request that the lender provide me with a fair and transparent opportunity to apply for its lending products. 
I am happy to provide a copy of my government-issued ID card and other documents that should satisfy lending requirements. 
Otherwise, the lender 's failure to provide explanation or an avenue for reconsideration of my application by allowing me to verify my identity unlawfully deprives me of the ability to utilize consumer credit, which is in violation of relevant state and federal laws. 
* * THE LENDER ALSO PULLED MY CREDIT REPORT WHICH SHOWS ON MY CREDIT AS A HARD PULL EVEN THOUGH IT WAS SUPPOSED TO BE A PREQUALIFICATION SOFT PULL. * * Please have the lender immediately process my credit application and issue the card I was already prequalified for!

recentComplaints.narrative:
I am filing a complaint regarding the closure of my XXXX credit card issued by Comenity. 

I have been a responsible cardholder for years. While traveling from New York to Arizona and preparing to fly to XXXX XXXX, my account was closed for suspicious activity without a clear explanation or meaningful opportunity to resolve the issue. 

The problem began when a replacement card was returned by the post office while my mail was on hold. I asked Comenity to resend it to the address where I am temporarily staying in XXXX. During that call, I could not be verified because my mobile number on file was outdated. I had attempted multiple times to update it online but was unable to do so. As a result, my account was blocked and I was told the matter would be escalated. My XXXX number was correct, but insufficient for verification purposes. 

I never received the promised letter with instructions. When I called back, I was told to email a copy of my ID and proof of address, which I did immediately. I received an automated reply stating they would respond within 30 days. Shortly afterward, I was informed when I called Comenity that my account had already been permanently closed. Escalations produced no explanation beyond suspicious activity, and I was told only that I could reapply. 

I am requesting : * A clear explanation of the specific reason for closure. 

* Confirmation of whether my identification was reviewed. 

* An opportunity to verify my identity and discuss reinstatement. 

It is troubling that a XXXX card intended for travelers was closed in part due to travel-related c...

recentComplaints.narrative:
Good Afternoon, I initially filed a complaint and received a response, but it appears I wasnt specific enough the first time, as I was given the same standard answer I received from XXXX customer service agents. 
To clarify : Yes, I reported the fraud to XXXX, and yes, they issued a partial credit. However, not all of the fraudulent charges were reimbursed, which is why I formally submitted a complaint. I also mailed a detailed letter explaining the situation ( attached ), and I filed a police report ( also attached ). 
Despite this, XXXX considers the matter resolved simply because they credited a portion of the charges. It is not resolved, because I did not authorize any of these transactions. Whether the issue was chip-related or not is irrelevantprotecting the customer is their responsibility. In this situation, I was not protected, and instead, I was made to feel as though I was at fault. That is unfair and unacceptable. 
This situation has dragged on for far too long and has caused significant emotional distress. XXXX informed me that the only way to reopen their investigation was to file a police report. I went to the police station three separate times, and each time I was dismissed because they considered the matter silly or unimportant. It wasnt until I broke down in frustration that the report was finally taken. 
I should not be held responsible simply because it is easier to hold me accountable than to identify the actual culprit. This is not fair in any way, shape, or form, and I am requesting a proper review and resolution of my case.

recentComplaints.narrative:
Continued Formal Dispute {$1700.00} Deferred Interest SECOND NOTICE OF BILLING ERROR Re : Account Ending XXXX CFPB Complaint ID : XXXX To Whom It May Concern : I am submitting this as a continued formal billing dispute under the Fair Credit Billing Act.

Your response does not resolve the core issue. While you state that statements were mailed and not returned, I did not receive periodic billing statements during the promotional period and did not have online portal access to monitor the promotional expiration date.

As a result, I was deprived of a meaningful opportunity to satisfy the promotional balance before retroactive deferred interest of approximately {$1700.00} was applied in a lump sum.

The assessment of retroactive interest under these circumstances constitutes a billing error under 15 U.S.C. 1666, as I did not receive accessible periodic statements reflecting the promotional expiration.

I again offer to immediately remit the full promotional principal balance upon removal of the {$1700.00} deferred interest charge.

If this matter is not resolved, I will pursue all remedies available under federal and Colorado law.

Please treat this as a continued formal dispute and provide written confirmation of your investigation timeline. 

Sincerely, XXXX XXXX

recentComplaints.narrative:
I am writing to formally dispute inaccurate information being reported on my credit file regarding my XXXX credit card account issued by Bread Financial. 

My credit reports currently reflect that this account is XXXX. However, upon contacting Bread Financial directly, I have been informed that the account is not open and is in a frozen status. 

This presents a material inconsistency. An account can not simultaneously be reported as open to consumer reporting agencies while the furnisher represents to the consumer that the account is not open. 

Pursuant to the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( 15 U.S.C. 1681s-2 ), furnishers of information have a duty to report accurate information. If the account has been closed or charging privileges permanently suspended, the reporting must accurately reflect that status. 

I am requesting that this account be investigated and corrected to reflect its true and accurate status. If the account remains open, then confirmation of that fact should be provided in writing.

I submitted written disputes regarding inaccurate and/or incomplete information appearing on my credit report. These disputes were sent pursuant to 15 U.S.C. 1681i ( a ), which requires a reasonable reinvestigation within 30 days of receipt of a consumer dispute.

As of today, more than 45 days have passed since my disputes were received. I have either received no response at all or responses that fail to demonstrate that a reasonable reinvestigation was conducted as required by law.

Specifically : Under 15 U.S.C. 1681i ( a ), the credit reporting agency must conduct a reasonable reinvestigation within 30 days. This has not been properly completed. 

Under FCRA 1681i ( a ) ( 5 ) ( A ), any information that can not be verified must be deleted. The disputed information remains on my credit report without documented verification.

Under 15 U.S.C. 1681i ( e ), agencies are required to properly compile and treat consumer complaints. The lack of meaningful response indicates noncompliance.

Under 15 U.S.C. 1692e ( 8 ), a debt collector may not communicate credit information known to be disputed without reporting the dispute. The collection agency has failed to properly mark the account as disputed.

recentComplaints.subIssue: Problem during payment process
recentComplaints.narrative:
I am filing this complaint because Comenity Capital Bank, operating through the Bread Financial app, utilizes a payment system that I believe is designed to prevent customers from making timely payments and avoid interest. 

The specific issue is with how they manage autopay on the XXXX XXXX XXXX credit card. Their mobile app and website do not allow a user to set up or schedule an autopay until a statement has officially closed and cleared. This creates a very small window of time each month where a customer is actually able to manage their payments. By the time the system unlocks the ability to pay, the deadline is so close that it is incredibly easy to miss it or for the payment to process late. 

I attempted to set up my payment ahead of time to be responsible, but the app physically barred me from doing so. Because of this technical restriction, I incurred interest charges on my balance. When I called their customer service department to explain that their own system prevented me from paying earlier and requested a one-time interest waiver, they flatly refused to help me. 

It feels like the bank is intentionally making it difficult to automate payments so they can collect more interest and fees. Most other credit card issuers allow you to set up autopay the moment you open the account. Restricting it to a tiny window after the statement clears is an unfair business practice. 

I would like the CFPB to look into this practice. I am requesting that Comenity Bank refund the interest charges that resulted from this systemic hurdle and fix their app so that customers can s...

recentComplaints.narrative:
I am filing this complaint because Capital One is reporting two charged-off accounts on my credit report that I dispute and that have not been properly validated. 

The accounts show balances of {$480.00} and {$140.00} and continue to be reported as charge-offs. I have disputed these accounts, yet they remain without Capital One providing competent evidence that I am legally obligated for these debts. 

Under the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( 15 U.S.C. 1681i ), a reasonable investigation is required when a consumer disputes information. Simply marking an account as verified without providing documentation does not satisfy this requirement. Additionally, 15 U.S.C. 1681e ( b ) requires maximum possible accuracy in credit reporting. Reporting unverified information violates this law.

Further, under the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act ( 15 U.S.C. 1692g ), I am entitled to proper validation of any alleged debt, including proof of contractual obligation.

If Capital One can not produce documentation proving I am legally responsible for these accounts, they must be deleted immediately.

I am requesting that the CFPB require Capital One and the credit reporting agencies to either provide proper legal validation or remove these accounts from my credit file. 

Sincerely, XXXX XXXX

recentComplaints.subIssue: Didn't receive advertised or promotional terms
recentComplaints.narrative:
I booked a hotel stay at XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ( XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ) through the Capital One Travel portal using my XXXX XXXX card on XX/XX/year>. 

Based on the cost representation which stated " total includes fees '', I reasonably understood that all mandatory hotel fees were included in the displayed total. 
After completing the booking, I received a confirmation email stating that a mandatory resort fee of {$50.00} per night would be charged at the property. 
For a 3-night stay, this amounts to : {$50.00} 3 nights = {$150.00} in additional mandatory charges This materially increases the total cost of the stay beyond what was represented at booking. 

The resort fee is not optional. It is a mandatory condition of staying at the property. 
Therefore, it is part of the true total cost. 

When I called in I was told that this information was included in the " Need to know '' section prior to booking. However, it was not and I have screenshots of other booking offers which also omit this information, indicating that there is an error. 

The booking page explicitly stated Total includes fees. That statement is inconsistent with a separate mandatory {$50.00} per night charge disclosed only after booking confirmation.

Following this, I have continued to be charged monthly interest of approximately {$70.00} {$88.00} at an APR of nearly 30 %.

I am filing this complaint against Synchrony Bank regarding a charged-off account that continues to be reported on my credit file despite multiple disputes and requests for validation over the past several months. 

I have made repeated requests for documentation to verify the accuracy of this account. However, Synchrony Bank has failed to provide sufficient evidence to support its reporting. The only information that has been referenced is that an item was delivered to my address. This does not constitute proper validation of a debt and does not establish contractual liability. 

Proof of delivery alone does not demonstrate that I entered into an agreement, authorized the account, or am legally responsible for the alleged balance. To date, Synchrony Bank has not provided : Any agreement or documentation showing that I entered into a contractual relationship A complete account history or billing statements supporting the balance An itemized breakdown explaining how the balance was calculated Despite this lack of documentation, Synchrony Bank continues to report this account as accurate and verified. This raises serious concerns regarding compliance with the Fair Credit Reporting Act, which requires furnishers to conduct a reasonable investigation and report only accurate and verifiable information. 

Repeatedly verifying this account without providing supporting documentation suggests that no meaningful investigation has been conducted.

I exercised my rights under the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act ( FDCPA ) by requesting verification of the alleged debt. The Proof : It is mathematically impossible for the Lender to have provided a 10-day notice for a payment amount that was only calculated and posted XXXX hours prior to the draft. 

2. Violation of Regulation Z & Deceptive Practices Branch and insurance department representatives have repeatedly claimed verbally and in writing that the XXXX charge " does not accrue interest. '' The Evidence : My XX/XX/year>, billing statement explicitly shows the {$420.00} charge was added to the " Principal '' column.

recentComplaints.narrative:
I am submitting this complaint regarding Upstarts inaccurate and false reporting of a charged-off account that is appearing on my consumer credit reports with XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX 

The account being reported by Upstart is not mine, and the balance being furnished reflects charges, account activity, and information that are not relevant to my knowledge or authorization. I do not recognize this account as belonging to me, nor do I recognize the balance or transaction history being reported. 

Despite this, Upstart continues to furnish this account as a valid charged-off debt, which I believe constitutes inaccurate, misleading, and unverifiable reporting in violation of the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( 15 U.S.C. 1681s-2 ). 

Upstart has failed to adequately ensure the accuracy, ownership, and completeness of the information being furnished before reporting it to the credit bureaus. This false reporting is causing ongoing financial harm, including materially impacting my ability to qualify for homeownership and mortgage financing. 

I have disputed this account directly and requested verification. At this time, the inaccurate reporting remains on my credit reports. 

I am requesting that the CFPB require Upstart to : Conduct a full investigation into the ownership and accuracy of this account Provide documented verification that this account belongs to me, including original application and authorization records Immediately delete the tradeline from all consumer credit reporting agencies if the account can not be fully verified as accurate and mine I am requesting relief in the...

recentComplaints.narrative:
I am reporting a systemic failure by Goldman Sachs ( Apple Card ) to properly investigate a billing error for 'Services Not Rendered ' ( 12 CFR 1026.13 ).

Despite three dispute attempts and clear documentation, the bank has repeatedly failed to conduct a 'reasonable investigation ' as required by the Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ). 

Timeline of Regulatory Failure : XX/XX/XXXX : Merchant provided written confirmation of a prorated refund ( {$950.00} ) due to business closure/lockout ( Attachment 02 ) XX/XX/XXXX : Initial dispute filed with the merchants refund promise as evidence. 

XX/XX/XXXX : Dispute denied. Goldman Sachs incorrectly cited an unrelated {$950.00} credit from XX/XX/XXXX ( 8 months prior to the incident ) as evidence that the refund was already issued ( Attachment 03 ). This is a gross administrative error. 

XX/XX/XXXX : Second dispute denied. Bank claimed the transaction was 'authorized, ' ignoring the fact that the dispute is for non-delivery of services for an authorized transaction ( FCBA 1026.13 ( a ) ( 3 ) ). 

XX/XX/XXXX : Third dispute denied claiming 'no evidence to substantiate, ' despite the submitted merchant emails and public record of the vendors XXXX bankruptcy ( Attachment 04 ). 

XX/XX/XXXX : A manager verbally admitted the previous rejection was 'incorrect ' but stated the only remedy was a fourth XXXX dispute cycle.

previousComplaints.narrative:
A collection account is appearing on my credit report that has not been properly validated. I exercised my rights under the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act ( FDCPA ) by requesting verification of the alleged debt. The creditor or collection agency has failed to provide any legitimate documentation demonstrating that the debt belongs to me, that the amount is correct, or that they have the legal authority to collect it.

recentComplaints.subIssue: Threatened or suggested your credit would be damaged
recentComplaints.narrative:
To Whom It May Concern, I am submitting this formal complaint regarding an improperly reported charged-off credit card account furnished by SoFi Bank , N.A., which reflects a profit-and-loss write-off / cancellation of debt, yet continues to be reported as an outstanding obligation on my consumer credit reports.

This complaint concerns violations of federal tax law, the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ), and applicable reporting standards. 

Account Information Creditor : SoFi Bank, N.A. 

Account Number : XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  Account Type : Credit Card Date Opened : XX/XX/XXXX Closed Date : XX/XX/XXXX Balance Reported : {$3800.00} High Balance : {$3800.00} Bureaus Reporting XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXdmission of Cancellation-of-Debt Event This tradeline is reported using explicit cancellation language, including : Charged off as bad debt Profit and loss write-off Unpaid balance reported as a loss by the credit grantor These statements constitute an identifiable cancellation-of-debt event under : 26 U.S.C. 6050P Treasury Regulation 1.6050P-1 Federal law requires that when a creditor cancels or discharges indebtedness of {$600.00} or more, the creditor must issue IRS Form 1099-C to the consumer and the Internal Revenue Service.

To date : No Form 1099-C has been issued or provided No explanation has been given justifying why a cancellation event is being reported for accounting purposes but not for tax purposes Inaccurate and Misleading Credit Reporting ( FCRA Violations ) A creditor can not legally treat a debt as canceled or written off for profit-and-loss purpose...

On XX/XX/year>, I contacted customer service and spoke to a representative for approximately XXXX minutes in reference to my account...

previousComplaints.narrative: I completed a loan application for a personal loan to consolidate debt this morning and signed it at XXXX XXXX. The document I signed very vaguely states that loans can be cancelled if given 'sufficient time ' but does not elaborate on what that timeframe is. at XXXX XXXX, I tried to cancel the loan and halt funding but according to SoFi XXXX the loan had somehow already reached the funding stage and was unable to be stopped. It was escalated through multiple levels of customer support via chat and then, when I tried to make a phone call, my calls kept getting dropped just as it started to ring through to a representative after spending XXXX minutes on hold. This feels wildly deceptive and I can't help but feel like it is a delay tactic to stall me until it actually is too late to halt the progress. I have tried to call some of the companies that the funds were meant to be dispersed to and they have told me they are not able to reject the payments that come in. I am incredibly frustrated and feel that SoFi is being both deceptive and entirely unhelpful in resolving this issue.
